name: Chevening Scholarship

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly living allowance set each year
other_benefits: Access to Chevening events and networking

dates.cycle_note: Usually opens in early August and closes in early November each year.

blockers.ielts_note: English test needed after you get a conditional offer.
blockers.admission_note: You apply to Chevening first. You must get your own university offers by mid-July.

documents_required: Proof of English (after a conditional offer)
application_steps: 1. Check you meet the work experience and degree rules.
2. Apply online during the open window.
3. Apply to at least three UK master's courses separately.
4. If shortlisted, attend an interview.
5. Get an unconditional university offer by the set date.

eligibility.min_academic: A bachelor's degree good enough for a UK master's.
other: You must return to Pakistan for at least 2 years after the scholarship.

warnings: Dates change every year. Check the official site before applying.
You must arrange your own university offers by the deadline.

name: Commonwealth Master's Scholarship

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly living allowance

dates.cycle_note: You apply through a nominating body such as HEC. Their deadline is usually late in the year.

blockers.ielts_note: Depends on the university you choose.
blockers.admission_note: You list university preferences. You do not need an offer before you apply.
unusual_documents: A nomination from an approved Pakistani nominating body

application_steps: 1. Check the list of approved nominating bodies for Pakistan.
2. Apply through that body during their window.
3. Complete the online Commonwealth application.
4. Wait for nomination and final selection.

other: You must be nominated. You cannot apply fully on your own.

warnings: You cannot apply directly. You must be nominated by an approved body such as HEC.
Dates change every year. Check the official site.

name: Gates Cambridge Scholarship
provider: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation (University of Cambridge)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a full maintenance allowance

dates.cycle_note: You apply at the same time as your Cambridge course. Deadlines are usually October to January depending on the course.

blockers.ielts_note: Cambridge needs proof of English for admission.

application_fee.note: Cambridge charges a course application fee. Some fee waivers exist.

blockers.admission_note: You apply to your Cambridge course and the scholarship at the same time.
unusual_documents: A personal statement written for Gates Cambridge

application_steps: 1. Choose an eligible Cambridge course.
2. Apply to the course and tick the Gates Cambridge funding box.
3. Submit all documents before the course deadline.
4. If shortlisted, attend an interview.

other: Very competitive. Only a small number are chosen each year.

warnings: Very competitive.
Deadlines depend on your course. Check the official site.

name: DAAD EPOS Development-Related Postgraduate Courses

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, about 934 EUR per month (approximate)

dates.cycle_note: Each course sets its own deadline. Many are between August and October.

blockers.ielts_note: Depends on the course. Many need IELTS or TOEFL. Some need German.
blockers.admission_note: You apply to the course and the scholarship together.
unusual_documents: Proof of at least 2 years of relevant work experience

application_steps: 1. Search the DAAD scholarship database for an EPOS course.
2. Check the course's own deadline and rules.
3. Apply to the course with the scholarship documents.
4. Wait for the course's selection.

eligibility.min_academic: A bachelor's degree, usually with good grades.
eligibility.work_experience: At least 2 years of relevant work experience.

warnings: Each course has its own rules and deadline.
Check the DAAD scholarship database for the exact course.

name: Erasmus Mundus Joint Master's (EMJM)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly allowance plus a contribution to living costs
other_benefits: Study in two or more countries

dates.cycle_note: Each master's programme sets its own deadline. Many close between December and February.

blockers.ielts_note: English proof is usually needed.
blockers.admission_note: You apply to the programme and its scholarship together.
unusual_documents: You often apply to a group of universities in one application.

application_steps: 1. Browse the Erasmus Mundus catalogue for a master's you like.
2. Open that programme's own website.
3. Apply on time with all documents.
4. Wait for the programme's decision.

eligibility.min_academic: A bachelor's degree relevant to the master's.

warnings: Each programme is run by a group of universities and has its own deadline.
Check the specific programme page.

name: Türkiye Bursları (Türkiye Scholarships)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly stipend that increases by study level
other_benefits: Free Turkish language course for one year

dates.cycle_note: Usually opens in January and closes in February each year.

blockers.ielts_note: IELTS is not required. Many programs are in Turkish or English.
blockers.admission_note: One application covers many universities and programs.

documents_required: Exam results if asked (varies by program)
application_steps: 1. Create an account on the official portal during the window.
2. Choose your study level and up to a set number of programs.
3. Upload your documents.
4. If shortlisted, attend an interview.

eligibility.age_limit: Undergraduate under 21, Master's under 30, PhD under 35 (approximate).
eligibility.min_academic: Undergraduate at least 70%, medical at least 90%, postgraduate at least 75% (approximate).

warnings: One application covers many universities and programs.
Dates change every year.

name: Stipendium Hungaricum

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly stipend (about 43,700 HUF for BS and MS)
other_benefits: Free dormitory or a housing contribution

dates.cycle_note: Usually opens in November and closes in mid-January.

blockers.ielts_note: Depends on the university and program.
blockers.admission_note: You pick programs in the online application. You do not need an offer first.

application_steps: 1. Check if you apply through HEC as the sending partner.
2. Fill in the online application during the window.
3. Choose up to two study programs.
4. Upload documents and submit.
5. Pass any entrance test the university sets.

eligibility.min_academic: Good academic record. Varies by program.
other: In Pakistan you may need to apply through HEC as the sending partner.

warnings: Flights are not covered.
In Pakistan you may need to apply through HEC. Check current rules.
Dates change every year.

name: Chinese Government Scholarship (CSC)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly stipend (about 2,500 CNY BS, 3,000 MS, 3,500 PhD)

dates.cycle_note: Windows vary by route. Many run between December and April.

blockers.ielts_note: Many English-taught programs accept a medium-of-instruction letter instead of IELTS. Rules vary by university.
blockers.admission_note: Some routes need a pre-admission letter from a Chinese university first.
unusual_documents: Foreigner Physical Examination Form (a medical check)
A study plan or research proposal

application_steps: 1. Pick a route: embassy, university, or provincial.
2. Choose a university and program.
3. Apply on the CSC online system and the university system.
4. Upload all documents before the deadline.

eligibility.age_limit: Undergraduate under 25, Master's under 35, PhD under 40 (varies by route).
eligibility.min_academic: Good academic record. Varies by university.

warnings: There are different routes (embassy, university, provincial). Rules differ.
Airfare is often not covered.
Some universities charge their own application fee. Check first.

name: MEXT Japanese Government Scholarship
provider: Government of Japan (Ministry of Education, MEXT)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly stipend (about 117,000 JPY for research students; varies)

dates.cycle_note: The embassy track for Pakistan usually runs April to June (the 2027 research round closed on 26 May 2026). The separate university-recommendation track runs later, with university deadlines around September to December. Check target universities directly.

blockers.ielts_note: IELTS is not strictly required. There are written tests and interviews. Some programs need English or Japanese proof.
blockers.admission_note: Embassy route: you apply first, then arrange university acceptance later.
unusual_documents: A field of study and research plan

documents_required: Field of study and research plan
application_steps: 1. Choose the embassy route or the university route.
2. For the embassy route, watch the Embassy of Japan in Pakistan.
3. Submit forms and sit any written tests.
4. Attend an interview if shortlisted.
5. Arrange university acceptance as guided.

eligibility.age_limit: Undergraduate around 24 or under; research route around 34 or under (varies).

warnings: There are two tracks: through the Japanese Embassy (Pakistan) or through a university.
Embassy track usually runs April to June. The 2027 research round closed on 26 May 2026.
University-recommendation track deadlines fall later, around September to December. Check universities directly.
Dates change every year. Watch the Embassy of Japan in Pakistan.

name: Global Korea Scholarship (GKS)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly stipend (about 900,000 KRW for graduate students)
other_benefits: One year of Korean language training

dates.cycle_note: The graduate route usually opens around February. The undergraduate route usually opens around September.

blockers.ielts_note: IELTS is not strictly required. A good TOPIK (Korean) or English score can help.
blockers.admission_note: You pick either the embassy track or the university track. Not both.
unusual_documents: A personal statement and study plan

application_steps: 1. Choose the embassy track or the university track.
2. Prepare the GKS forms and documents.
3. Apply during the correct window for your track.
4. Pass the review and any interview.

eligibility.age_limit: Usually under 25 for undergraduate; graduate has a higher limit (varies).
eligibility.min_academic: Usually a CGPA of about 80% or higher, or top 20% of class.

warnings: There are two tracks: embassy and university. You can only pick one.
Dates change every year.

name: Fulbright Foreign Student Program (Pakistan)
provider: US Government (managed by USEFP in Pakistan)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly living stipend

dates.cycle_note: In Pakistan it has usually opened around February to March and closed around May.

blockers.ielts_note: You need TOEFL (not IELTS) and usually the GRE. USEFP helps arrange these.
blockers.admission_note: USEFP helps place you at a US university. You do not need an offer first.

application_steps: 1. Apply through USEFP during the open window.
2. Take the GRE and TOEFL (USEFP helps arrange these).
3. Attend interviews if shortlisted.
4. USEFP helps place you at a suitable US university.

eligibility.min_academic: A four-year bachelor's degree (16 years of education) with strong grades.
other: You must return to Pakistan for at least 2 years after the program.

warnings: US-funded programs can change with US government decisions. Confirm the program is running this year.
You apply through USEFP in Pakistan, not directly to a US university.

name: Australia Awards Scholarships

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a living allowance

dates.cycle_note: Windows vary by country. For many it opens around February and closes around April.

blockers.ielts_note: IELTS or an accepted English test is required.
blockers.admission_note: You apply for the award. Placement is handled as part of the process.

application_steps: 1. Check if Pakistan is a participating country this round.
2. Apply online during the open window.
3. Provide English test results.
4. Attend an interview if shortlisted.

eligibility.work_experience: Some work experience is usually expected.
other: You must return home for at least 2 years after study.

warnings: Pakistan may not be a participating country every year. Confirm eligibility first.
Dates change every year.

name: Swedish Institute Scholarships for Global Professionals (SISGP)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly living grant (about 12,000 SEK)
other_benefits: Membership of the SI alumni network

dates.cycle_note: You first apply to Swedish universities (around January). The scholarship application is usually a short window in February.

blockers.ielts_note: English proof such as IELTS is needed for university admission.

application_fee.note: You pay a university application fee first. The scholarship does not remove this fee.

blockers.admission_note: You must apply to eligible master's programmes first. Only then can you be considered.
unusual_documents: Proof of at least 3,000 hours of work experience
A CV in the required SI format

documents_required: Proof of English (IELTS or accepted test)
application_steps: 1. Apply to eligible master's programmes and pay the university fee.
2. Wait for the scholarship window in February.
3. Submit the SI scholarship application and documents.
4. Wait for the SI decision.

eligibility.work_experience: At least 3,000 hours of work, volunteering, or leadership experience.

warnings: You must pay the Swedish university application fee yourself.
You must apply to universities first, then the scholarship.
Dates change every year.

name: Islamic Development Bank (IsDB) Scholarship Programme

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly stipend (amount varies by country and level)

dates.cycle_note: Windows vary by year and program. Often early in the year.

blockers.ielts_note: Depends on the country and university you choose.
blockers.admission_note: Some categories need a university admission letter. Rules vary.
unusual_documents: You may need to commit to work in a development field after study.

application_steps: 1. Read the current IsDB scholarship call.
2. Check which category fits you (BS, MS, or PhD).
3. Apply online during the window.
4. Provide any admission proof the category needs.

eligibility.min_academic: Good academic record. Varies by level.
other: For students from IsDB member countries. Pakistan is a member country.

warnings: Rules and benefits change by country, level, and year. Read the current call carefully.
Study country and university vary.

name: Manaaki New Zealand Scholarship

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a living allowance

dates.cycle_note: Windows vary by country. Often around February to March.

blockers.ielts_note: IELTS or an accepted English test is required.
blockers.admission_note: You apply for the scholarship. Placement is part of the process.

application_steps: 1. Check if Pakistan is an eligible country this round.
2. Apply online during the open window.
3. Provide English test results.
4. Attend an interview if shortlisted.

eligibility.work_experience: Some work experience is usually expected.
other: You must return home for at least 2 years after study.

warnings: Pakistan may not be an eligible country every year. Confirm eligibility first.
Dates change every year.

name: Eiffel Excellence Scholarship
provider: Campus France (French Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly allowance (about 1,181 EUR for master's)

dates.cycle_note: Your French institution must apply for you, usually around December to January.

blockers.ielts_note: Depends on the program. Some are taught in French, some in English.
blockers.admission_note: You cannot apply yourself. A French institution must nominate you.

application_steps: 1. Apply to a French institution that takes part in Eiffel.
2. Ask them to nominate you for the Eiffel scholarship.
3. The institution submits your file to Campus France.
4. Wait for the results.
warnings: You cannot apply directly. A French university must nominate you.
The scholarship gives a living allowance but does not pay tuition.
Dates change every year.

name: Swiss Government Excellence Scholarships

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly stipend (about 1,920 CHF for PhD)

dates.cycle_note: Deadlines depend on your country. They often fall between September and December.

blockers.ielts_note: Depends on the university and language of study.
blockers.admission_note: For research scholarships you must find a host professor first.

application_steps: 1. Check if Pakistan is on the eligible country list this year.
2. Find a host professor at a Swiss university.
3. Apply through the route for your country before the deadline.
4. Wait for the selection.

other: Eligibility and the levels offered depend on your country and change each year.

warnings: Eligibility depends on your country and changes each year. Check if Pakistan is listed.
For research scholarships you must find a host professor first.
Dates change every year.

name: Government of Ireland International Education Scholarship

funding_details.monthly_stipend: A 10,000 EUR stipend for one year (a contribution toward living costs)
other_benefits: Full tuition fee waiver for one year

dates.cycle_note: Usually opens early in the year and closes around March.

blockers.ielts_note: English proof is usually needed for admission.
blockers.admission_note: You apply for the scholarship and to a course. Check the current rules.

application_steps: 1. Apply online during the open window.
2. Show how you meet the eligibility rules.
3. Wait for the selection results.
warnings: The scholarship funds one year only.
The 10,000 EUR is a contribution, not full living costs.
Dates change every year.

name: Ernst Mach Grant (OeAD)
provider: OeAD (Austria's Agency for Education and Internationalisation)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly grant (about 1,150 EUR)

dates.cycle_note: Deadlines vary by programme. A common deadline is around the start of March.

blockers.ielts_note: Depends on the programme and language of study.
blockers.admission_note: Some grants need an invitation or a host institution in Austria.

application_steps: 1. Search the grants.at database for an Ernst Mach programme.
2. Check the programme's own deadline and rules.
3. Apply online with your documents.
4. Wait for the selection.
warnings: Each programme has its own rules and deadline.
Airfare is usually not covered.
Dates change every year.

name: Italian Government MAECI Scholarship

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly allowance (about 900 EUR)

dates.cycle_note: Usually opens in spring and closes around May.

blockers.ielts_note: Depends on the course. Many need English or Italian proof.
blockers.admission_note: A tuition waiver depends on the university, not the scholarship.

application_steps: 1. Apply online during the open window.
2. Choose your study level and programme.
3. Upload your documents.
4. Wait for the results.
warnings: Tuition waiver is not guaranteed. It depends on the university.
Airfare is not covered.
Dates change every year.

name: VLIR-UOS Scholarships

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly allowance

dates.cycle_note: Each programme sets its own deadline. Many close between January and March.

blockers.ielts_note: English proof is usually needed.
blockers.admission_note: You apply to an eligible master's programme and the scholarship together.
unusual_documents: Proof you are from an eligible country

application_steps: 1. Pick an eligible master's programme in Flanders.
2. Check that Pakistan is on the eligible country list.
3. Apply to the programme and the scholarship before the deadline.
4. Wait for the selection.

other: For students from selected countries. Pakistan is on the eligible list for many programmes.

warnings: Only certain master's programmes are covered.
Check the eligible programme and country list each year.
Dates change every year.

name: Finland Scholarships

dates.cycle_note: You apply through the university's master's admission, usually around December to January.

blockers.ielts_note: English proof is needed for admission.
blockers.admission_note: Each university runs its own Finland Scholarship. You apply to the master's programme.

application_steps: 1. Find a master's programme that offers the Finland Scholarship.
2. Apply to the programme through the admission system.
3. Top applicants are offered the scholarship.
4. Wait for the admission and scholarship result.

other: For non-EU/EEA students who pay tuition.

warnings: Each university runs its own version. Rules differ.
It covers first-year tuition and a relocation grant, not full living costs.
Dates change every year.

name: Danish Government Scholarship
provider: Danish universities (Ministry of Higher Education and Science)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Sometimes a grant toward living costs (varies)

dates.cycle_note: You are considered when you apply to a master's programme, usually around January to March.

blockers.ielts_note: English proof is needed for admission.
blockers.admission_note: You do not apply separately. Universities choose from their applicants.

application_steps: 1. Apply to an eligible master's programme in Denmark.
2. The university considers you for the scholarship.
3. Wait for the admission and funding decision.

other: For non-EU/EEA students who pay tuition.

warnings: You cannot apply separately. Universities choose from programme applicants.
Support varies — it may be full or partial tuition, sometimes with a living grant.
Dates change every year.

name: Romanian Government Scholarship
provider: Government of Romania (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly allowance (varies by study level)
other_benefits: One free year of Romanian language

dates.cycle_note: Usually opens early in the year and closes around March.

blockers.ielts_note: IELTS is usually not required. Many programmes are in Romanian, with a free language year.
blockers.admission_note: You apply through the Romanian Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
unusual_documents: You may need to study one year of Romanian first

application_steps: 1. Apply online during the open window.
2. Choose your study level and field.
3. Upload your documents.
4. Wait for the selection.
warnings: Many programmes are taught in Romanian.
Airfare is not covered.
Dates change every year.

name: Polish NAWA Scholarship Programmes
provider: NAWA (Polish National Agency for Academic Exchange)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly allowance (varies by programme)

dates.cycle_note: NAWA runs several programmes, each with its own deadline. Many run early in the year.

blockers.ielts_note: Depends on the programme and language of study.
blockers.admission_note: Rules depend on the specific NAWA programme.

application_steps: 1. Browse the NAWA website for a programme that fits you.
2. Check that it accepts Pakistani students.
3. Apply online before that programme's deadline.
4. Wait for the results.

other: NAWA runs several programmes. Check which ones accept Pakistani students.

warnings: NAWA is an umbrella for many programmes. Rules and eligibility differ.
Not every programme is open to Pakistani students. Check each one.
Dates change every year.

name: National Scholarship Programme of the Slovak Republic
provider: National Scholarship Programme of the Slovak Republic (SAIA)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly scholarship to cover living costs

dates.cycle_note: There are usually two rounds a year, with deadlines around the end of April and the end of October.

blockers.ielts_note: Depends on the host university and language of study.
blockers.admission_note: You usually need an invitation or acceptance from a Slovak university first.
unusual_documents: An invitation letter from a Slovak university

documents_required: Invitation letter from a Slovak university
application_steps: 1. Contact a Slovak university and get an acceptance or invitation.
2. Prepare your study or research plan.
3. Apply online before the round's deadline.
4. Wait for the selection.
warnings: The scholarship covers living costs, not tuition.
You usually need a Slovak university to accept you first.
Dates change every year.

name: Open Doors: Russian Scholarship Project
provider: Association of Global Universities (Open Doors), Russia

dates.cycle_note: You register in the autumn and take the online contest around December to February.

blockers.ielts_note: IELTS is not required. The contest is in English or Russian.
blockers.admission_note: You must win the Open Doors contest to get a tuition-free place.
unusual_documents: You must win the Open Doors online contest

documents_required: Register an account on the Open Doors site
application_steps: 1. Register on the Open Doors website in the autumn.
2. Take the online contest for your subject.
3. If you win, pick a Russian university for a tuition-free place.
4. Complete admission with that university.

other: Open to international students who win the contest.

warnings: This is a competition. Only winners get a tuition-free place.
It usually covers tuition only, not living costs or airfare.
Check current conditions before applying.

name: Brunei Darussalam Government Scholarship
provider: Government of Brunei Darussalam (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly personal allowance (about BND 500)

dates.cycle_note: Usually opens early in the year and closes around February. Dates change each year — confirm officially.

blockers.ielts_note: English proof is usually required. Confirm with the program.
blockers.admission_note: One application to the government scheme. You pick your programs.

application_steps: 1. Read the current Brunei Government Scholarship call.
2. Pick your study level and programs.
3. Apply online during the window.
4. Wait for the selection.

eligibility.age_limit: Usually under 25 for undergraduate and under 35 for postgraduate (approximate).

warnings: One application covers the scheme.
Dates change every year. Confirm officially.

name: Taiwan ICDF Scholarship
provider: Taiwan International Cooperation and Development Fund (TaiwanICDF)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly allowance

dates.cycle_note: Usually opens around January and closes around mid-March. Dates change each year.

blockers.ielts_note: English proof is required. Some programs accept a medium-of-instruction letter.
blockers.admission_note: You apply through the ICDF system and pick partner-university programs.

application_steps: 1. Check if Pakistan is on the current ICDF eligible-country list.
2. Choose an eligible program.
3. Apply through the ICDF online system.
4. Wait for the selection.

other: For citizens of TaiwanICDF partner countries. Pakistan is usually not on the list.

warnings: Pakistan is usually not on the Taiwan ICDF eligible-country list. Check the current list before applying.
Dates change every year.

name: Taiwan MOE Scholarship

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly stipend
other_benefits: Tuition subsidy (up to a set cap)

dates.cycle_note: Usually opens around February and closes around March, through a Taiwan representative office. Dates change each year.

blockers.ielts_note: English or Chinese proof may be required, depending on the program.
blockers.admission_note: You apply through a Taiwan representative office.

application_steps: 1. Check whether a Taiwan representative office serves your country.
2. Prepare your documents.
3. Apply through that office during the window.
4. Wait for the selection.

other: Applications go through a Taiwan representative office. Pakistan does not have one.

warnings: Taiwan has no representative office in Pakistan, so this is usually hard to apply for from Pakistan. Confirm current arrangements.
Airfare is not covered.
Dates change every year.

name: Hong Kong PhD Fellowship Scheme
provider: Research Grants Council of Hong Kong (RGC)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly stipend (about HK$28,100)

dates.cycle_note: You first submit an initial application to the scheme, usually by early December. Then you apply to the universities. Dates change each year.

blockers.ielts_note: English proof is usually required for admission.
blockers.admission_note: You submit an initial application to the scheme first, then apply to universities.

application_steps: 1. Submit an initial application to the fellowship scheme by the deadline.
2. Get a reference number.
3. Apply to your chosen universities using it.
4. Wait for interviews and decisions.
warnings: Very competitive.
Tuition arrangements depend on the university. Confirm with your host university.
Dates change every year.

name: Singapore International Graduate Award (SINGA)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly stipend (about S$2,700, rising to S$3,200)

dates.cycle_note: There are usually two intakes a year (January and August), each with a window a few months earlier. Confirm current dates.

blockers.ielts_note: English proof (IELTS/TOEFL) is usually required unless your degree was in English.
blockers.admission_note: One application covers several partner universities.

application_steps: 1. Prepare your documents and research interests.
2. Apply online during the intake window.
3. Choose partner universities and supervisors.
4. Wait for interviews and decisions.

eligibility.min_academic: A strong academic record in science or engineering.

warnings: Two intakes a year. Check the current window.
Very competitive.
Dates change every year.

name: Indonesia KNB Scholarship

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly living allowance

dates.cycle_note: Usually opens early in the year and closes around March. Dates change each year.

blockers.ielts_note: English proof is usually required. Some programs are taught in Indonesian.
blockers.admission_note: One application covers the scheme. You pick partner universities.

application_steps: 1. Check the current KNB call and eligible universities.
2. Prepare your documents.
3. Apply online during the window.
4. Wait for the selection.

other: For students from developing countries. Pakistan is usually eligible.

warnings: Some programs are taught in Indonesian.
Dates change every year.

name: Malaysia International Scholarship (MIS)
provider: Government of Malaysia (Ministry of Higher Education)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly living allowance (about RM 1,500)

dates.cycle_note: The window moves each year. The 2026 call ran 4 March to 3 April. The 2025 call ran 10 to 30 June. It is usually announced between February and June.

blockers.ielts_note: English proof (IELTS/TOEFL) is usually required.
blockers.admission_note: Mostly research-based. You usually arrange a place or supervisor at a Malaysian university.

application_steps: 1. Watch for the current MIS call (it moves each year).
2. Prepare a research proposal.
3. Apply online during the window.
4. Wait for the selection.
warnings: Mostly for research-based Master's and PhD.
The application window moves each year. Check the current call.
Airfare is not covered.

name: MBZUAI Graduate Scholarship
provider: Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence (MBZUAI)
universities: Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly stipend

dates.cycle_note: Applications usually open in the second half of the year for the next intake. Check the current deadline.

blockers.admission_note: Admitted students are fully funded — admission and funding come together.

application_steps: 1. Choose an AI or related program.
2. Prepare your statement and references.
3. Apply online before the deadline.
4. Wait for the decision.

eligibility.min_academic: A strong academic record in a related field.

warnings: This is an AI-focused university. Programs are in AI and related fields.
Competitive.
Dates change every year.

name: Khalifa University Scholarship

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly stipend

dates.cycle_note: Applications usually open a few months before each intake (Fall and Spring). Check the current deadline.

blockers.ielts_note: English proof (IELTS/TOEFL) is required.
blockers.admission_note: Strong applicants get tuition and a stipend with admission.

application_steps: 1. Choose a graduate program.
2. Prepare your documents.
3. Apply online before the deadline.
4. Wait for the funded-admission decision.
warnings: Funding is competitive and merit-based.
Dates change every year.

name: HBKU Scholarship

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly stipend for eligible students

dates.cycle_note: Applications usually open in autumn for the next year. Check the current deadline.

blockers.ielts_note: English proof (IELTS/TOEFL) is required.
blockers.admission_note: Funding depends on the program and your profile.

application_steps: 1. Choose a graduate program.
2. Prepare your documents.
3. Apply online before the deadline.
4. Wait for the admission and funding decision.
warnings: Funding depends on the program and your profile. Confirm what is covered.

name: KAUST Fellowship
provider: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST)
universities: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, an annual living stipend (about US$20,000–30,000)

dates.cycle_note: KAUST reviews applications on a rolling basis, with priority deadlines before the Fall intake. Apply early.

blockers.ielts_note: English proof (IELTS/TOEFL) is required. Some applicants with English-medium degrees may be exempt.
blockers.admission_note: All admitted students receive the KAUST Fellowship.

application_steps: 1. Choose a program in science, engineering, or computing.
2. Prepare your statement and references.
3. Apply online (rolling; apply early).
4. Wait for the funded-admission decision.
warnings: Focused on science, engineering, and technology.
Rolling admissions — apply early.
Dates change each year.

name: ADB–Japan Scholarship Program
provider: Asian Development Bank (with the Government of Japan)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, a monthly subsistence and housing allowance

dates.cycle_note: You apply through a designated partner university. Deadlines vary by university and program — check each one.

blockers.ielts_note: Depends on the partner university and program.
blockers.admission_note: You apply to a designated partner university first, then for the scholarship through it.
unusual_documents: You must apply through a designated institution

documents_required: Proof of admission or application to a designated university
application_steps: 1. Pick a designated ADB-JSP partner university and program.
2. Apply to that university.
3. Ask about the ADB-JSP scholarship through it.
4. Meet that university's deadline.

other: For citizens of ADB borrowing member countries. Pakistan is a member.

warnings: You apply through a designated partner university, not to ADB directly.
Deadlines vary by university and program. Check each one.
Not all fields or universities are covered.

name: Vanier Canada Graduate Scholarships

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, CAD 50,000 per year for up to three years
other_benefits: A large annual stipend for up to 3 years

dates.cycle_note: You must be nominated by a Canadian university. University deadlines are usually in the autumn (often September to November).

blockers.ielts_note: English or French proof is usually required for admission.
blockers.admission_note: You must be nominated by a Canadian university. Contact them early.

application_steps: 1. Contact a Canadian university early about a PhD place.
2. Ask to be nominated for the Vanier scholarship.
3. Prepare your research and leadership documents.
4. The university submits your nomination.
5. Wait for the result.
warnings: You cannot apply directly. A Canadian university must nominate you.
It is a large stipend but does not pay tuition.
Dates change every year.

name: Lester B. Pearson International Scholarship

other_benefits: Full residence support for four years

dates.cycle_note: Your school must nominate you first (usually by November). Then you apply, usually by mid-January.

blockers.ielts_note: English proof is required unless you qualify for a waiver.
blockers.admission_note: You must apply to the University of Toronto and be nominated by your school.

application_steps: 1. Ask your school to nominate you (usually by November).
2. Apply to the University of Toronto for admission.
3. Complete the Pearson steps by the deadline.
4. Wait for the result.

other: Undergraduate study only.

warnings: Undergraduate only.
Your school must nominate you first.
Very competitive.

name: Knight-Hennessy Scholars

other_benefits: Travel allowance for up to 3 years

Very competitive.

name: Rhodes Scholarship (Pakistan)

funding_details.monthly_stipend: Yes, an annual stipend

dates.cycle_note: The Pakistan constituency runs its own process. Applications usually open mid-year and close around late summer. Confirm the current dates.

eligibility.age_limit: Usually at least 18 and not older than about 24, with some flexibility. Check the Pakistan criteria.

dates.cycle_note: You are considered automatically when you apply to an eligible Oxford course. Meet the course's deadline, usually in December or January.

warnings: No separate application — you apply to an Oxford course and are considered automatically.
